title: fraxinus bornmuelleri
intro: Fraxinus bornmuelleri, also known as the Bornmueller's ash, is a species of ash tree native to the mountains of North Africa. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus bornmuelleri lie in its bark, which contains a compound called lupeol, a triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Fraxinus bornmuelleri is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
